name: Soba Noodles With Quick-pickled Mushrooms
intro: Place the ancho chile in a small heatproof bowl and pour in boiling water just

ingredients.item: 1¼-inch/3-cm piece fresh ginger (⅓ oz/10 g), peeled and cut into very thin strips
oz/150 g buna-shimeji (brown beech) mushrooms, separated into individual mushrooms
small carrots, peeled and cut into very thin matchsticks (¾ cup/100 g)
cup/20 g salad cress or another cress or tender leaf
green onion, white and green parts, trimmed and very thinly sliced (2½ tbsp/15 g)
nori (dried seaweed) sheets (8 inches/20 cm square) cut into ⅜ by 1½-inch/1 by 4-cm strips
oz/50 g snow peas (about 15), finely shredded salt

method: In a small saucepan, mix together the vinegar, sugar, ginger, and ¾ teaspoon salt. Gently warm to dissolve the sugar, remove from the heat, and add the
mushrooms. Set aside to cool, stirring occasionally.
Blanch the carrots in a small saucepan of boiling water for 30 seconds. Drain, refresh under cold water, drain again, and dry well.

Throw the noodles into a pan with plenty of boiling salted water and cook for about 7 minutes, or as instructed on the package, until al dente. Drain, refresh under cold water, and set aside in a colander to drain.

Just before serving, in a large bowl, combine the mushrooms and their liquid, carrots, noodles, radishes, cress, green onion, chile, cilantro, nori, oil, sesame seeds, and snow peas. Toss together gently and serve.
